Markdy

An open-source animation DSL engine for blog posts.

→ Playground: Two rivals, one heart

Spec example

The canonical MarkdyScript scene from the spec, running live in the browser.

▶ markdy
View DSL source 
scene width=800 height=400 fps=30 bg=white

asset pepe = image("/memes/pepe.webp")
asset cat  = image("/memes/cat.png")
asset fire = icon("lucide:flame")

actor p     = sprite(pepe) at (100,250) scale 0.4
actor c     = sprite(cat)  at (600,250) scale 0.4
actor title = text("Ship it") at (320,80) size 48

@0.0: p.enter(from=left, dur=0.8)
@1.0: p.say("bruh", dur=1.0)
@2.0: p.move(to=(300,250), dur=1.0, ease=inout)
@3.0: p.throw(fire, to=c, dur=0.8)
@4.0: c.shake(intensity=3, dur=0.5)
@4.6: c.fade_out(dur=0.4)
@5.2: title.fade_in(dur=0.5)

Text animation

A minimal entrance sequence using only text actors.

▶ markdy
View DSL source 
scene width=600 height=200 fps=30 bg=#1a1a2e

actor headline = text("Hello, MarkdyScript") at (50,90) size 36 opacity 0
actor sub      = text("An animation DSL for the web") at (50,140) size 18 opacity 0

@0.2: headline.fade_in(dur=0.6)
@1.0: sub.fade_in(dur=0.5)
@2.0: headline.move(to=(80,90), dur=1.0, ease=out)
